title: Deconstruction: the qualitative methodology for the analysis of sustainability assessment tools of agri-system
author: Bonisoli, Galdeano-Gómez, et al.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: This methodology relies on three basic assumptions:

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: First, in a SAT nothing is casual. This methodology considers that all conceptual tools are built using a precise logic that is functional to SAT purpose.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Second, the logic behind the SAT is underpinned by a precise philosophical understanding.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Third, there is not a “best” philosophical view, thus the purpose of the analyst is not to judge the different concepts of sustainability but to reveal the concept behind the instrument.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Deconstruction has not a formal set of steps for its application but can uses different tactics. A possible process could be (see Chart 1):Chart 1Deconstruction process.Chart 1

steps_raw.title: Author analysis
steps_raw.instruction: This tactic considers to investigate other article of the same authors to check if there are relationships among different studies. It is possible that the same author who in an article presents a new SAT, in another study is claiming the need for a certification of sustainable product similar to the certification of organic product; thus, the aim of the SAT is probably to be the instrument to evaluate a future certification in a project of a certification business.
